section_miscategorizations.text: BMP's have been approximately 3,000 in March and are currently around 6,000.
section_miscategorizations.reason: These are objective lab values and belong in Findings rather than in the patient history narrative.

section_miscategorizations.text: Her last A1C was 5.5 in March.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a specific prior lab result; while mentioned in history, it is more appropriately organized under objective findings.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports leg cramps, which she attributed to being cold and drinking pickle juice.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Leg cramps are a patient-reported symptom; placing them under exam Findings blurs the distinction between subjective symptoms and objective exam.

Diabetes is a chronic comorbidity rather than a new diagnostic impression; it would typically be documented in past medical history/HPI, though listing it among active problems in Assessment is not seriously misleading.
